zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Smith於1624年10月9日出生在英國諾福爾可諾維其時，她的父親Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield26歲，母親Dorothy Cotton25歲。她於大約1641年與Samuel Hale結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1712年1月19日在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年87歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德格拉斯頓波瑞格拉斯頓波瑞 特格林 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Smith于1624年10月9日出生在英国諾福爾可諾維其时，她的父亲Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield26岁，母亲Dorothy Cotton25岁。她于大约1641年与Samuel Hale结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1712年1月19日在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年87岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德格拉斯頓波瑞格拉斯頓波瑞 特格林 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

historicEvents.description: In 1636, 30 families were settled in Pyaug, a tract of land belonging to Wethersfield

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
Oldest grave seen in the Memorials list.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Smith was born on 9 October 1624, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, her father, Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield, was 26 and her mother, Dorothy Cotton, was 25. She married Samuel Hale about 1641. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 19 January 1712, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 87, and was buried in Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Smith est née le 9 octobre 1624, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Cotton, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Samuel Hale environ 1641. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 janvier 1712, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterrée à Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Smith am 9. Oktober 1624 in Norwich, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield 26 und ihre Mutter Dorothy Cotton 25. Sie hat um 1641 Samuel Hale geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 19. Januar 1712 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Smith è nata il il 9 ottobre 1624 nel Norwich, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Dorothy Cotton, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Samuel Hale circa 1641. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 19 gennaio 1712, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolta a Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Smithが1624年10月9日にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで生まれた時，父Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfieldは26歳，母Dorothy Cottonは25歳であった。約1641年にSamuel Haleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1712年1月19日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで87歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードグラストンブリーグラストンブリー センターグレン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Smith(이)가 1624년 10월 9일 영국 노르폴크 노위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Cotton(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1641년 Samuel Hale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1712년 1월 19일 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 글라스토버이 글라스토버이 세터 그린 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Smith nasceu em 9 de outubro de 1624, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Cotton, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Samuel Hale aproximadamente 1641. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 19 de janeiro de 1712, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, com 87 anos, e foi sepultada em Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Smith родилась 9 октября 1624 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Cotton, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Samuel Hale около 1641 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 19 января 1712 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 87 лет и была похоронена в местности грин кеметири, гластонбури кентир, гластонбури, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Smith nació el 9 de octubre de 1624, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Rev. Henry Smith of Wethersfield, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Dorothy Cotton, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Samuel Hale aproximadamente 1641. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 19 de enero de 1712, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultada en Green Cemetery, Glastonbury Center, Glastonbury, Hartford, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
